# psychnets

> **Clean-room, self-certifying estimation of cross-sectional
> psychometric network models in base R.**
>
> — by [**Mohammed Saqr**](https://saqr.me)

`psychnets` estimates the cross-sectional models of psychometric network
analysis — correlation and partial-correlation networks, the
EBIC-regularized Gaussian graphical model (the graphical lasso) together
with its nonparanormal and unregularized stepwise variants, the
information-filtering graphs (the Triangulated Maximally Filtered Graph
and the Local–Global inverse covariance), relative-importance networks,
the Ising model for binary data, and the mixed graphical model —
reimplemented from first principles in base R. These models are
conventionally fitted through packages that wrap compiled numerical
kernels: `qgraph` and `bootnet` route the graphical lasso to the Fortran
package `glasso`, while `IsingFit` and `mgm` route their nodewise
regressions to `glmnet`. `psychnets` calls none of these packages and
none of the compiled solvers beneath them; its only imports are base R’s
own `stats`, `parallel`, `graphics`, and `grDevices`.

Every regularized fit returns a **correctness certificate**: the
stationarity (KKT) residual of the convex objective the estimator solves
— a quantity that is near zero at the optimum and is computed with no
reference to any external solver.

## Design properties

`psychnets` is constructed so that a fitted network is:

- **Transparent** — the estimator, including the numerical solve, is
  written in the language of the analysis; no step is delegated to a
  Fortran or C kernel that is illegible from R.
- **Reproducible** — a result does not depend on a compiled binary’s
  convergence tolerance fixed at build time, a setting the analyst does
  not control.
- **Dependency-light** — no `glasso`, `glmnet`, `qgraph`, or `Matrix`,
  and none of the recursive `Rcpp` / `RcppArmadillo` tree those pull in.
- **Auditable** — the certificate reports how far a fit lies from the
  optimum of its own objective, so correctness is verifiable from the
  fit itself rather than by re-estimation in a second package.

Because `psychnets` solves each objective to its optimum and applies the
textbook extended Bayesian information criterion, it does not reproduce
the spurious edges that a loosely converged reference solver can select.

## Installation

``` r

# from CRAN (once released)
install.packages("psychnets")

# development version
# install.packages("pak")
pak::pak("mohsaqr/psychnets")
```

## A worked example

`psychnets` estimates an EBIC-regularized Gaussian graphical model from
data or a correlation matrix and reports its certificate on printing.
The example uses `SRL_GPT`, one of the self-regulated-learning datasets
shipped with the package (five MSLQ constructs).

``` r

library(psychnets)

net <- ebic_glasso(SRL_GPT)   # EBIC graphical lasso of the five MSLQ constructs
net                           # edges, lambda, gamma, and the KKT residual
#> <psychnet> glasso network
#>   nodes: 5   edges: 10   (undirected)
#>   lambda: 0.00861   gamma: 0.5
#>   optimality (KKT residual): 2.21e-10

net_centralities(net)         # strength and expected influence, tidy
#>   node  strength expected_influence
#> 1  CSU 1.1984512         1.19845117
#> 2   IV 1.0012765         1.00127649
#> 3   SE 0.8492185         0.84921854
#> 4   SR 1.2314317         0.53172852
#> 5   TA 0.6082238        -0.09147935
```

The network is drawn through
[`cograph::splot()`](https://sonsoles.me/cograph/reference/splot.html):

``` r

cograph::splot(net, layout = "spring", edge_labels = FALSE, legend = TRUE)
```

![SRL_GPT regulation network](reference/figures/README-network-1.png)

The certificate is available directly, so the distance of the fit from
the unique optimum of its objective can be read without an external
reference:

``` r

glasso_kkt(net$precision, net$cor_matrix, net$lambda)
#> [1] 2.213367e-10
```

## Models and methods

The estimators, each fitted in base R and — where regularized —
self-certified:

| Verb | Model | Data |
|----|----|----|
| [`cor_network()`](https://pak.dynasite.org/psychnets/reference/cor_network.md) | correlation network (with significance thresholding) | continuous |
| [`pcor_network()`](https://pak.dynasite.org/psychnets/reference/pcor_network.md) | partial-correlation network | continuous |
| [`ebic_glasso()`](https://pak.dynasite.org/psychnets/reference/ebic_glasso.md) | EBIC-regularized Gaussian graphical model (graphical lasso) | continuous |
| [`huge_network()`](https://pak.dynasite.org/psychnets/reference/huge_network.md) | nonparanormal Gaussian graphical model | continuous |
| [`ggm_modselect()`](https://pak.dynasite.org/psychnets/reference/ggm_modselect.md) | unregularized stepwise Gaussian graphical model | continuous |
| [`tmfg_network()`](https://pak.dynasite.org/psychnets/reference/tmfg_network.md) | Triangulated Maximally Filtered Graph | continuous |
| [`logo_network()`](https://pak.dynasite.org/psychnets/reference/logo_network.md) | Local–Global sparse inverse covariance | continuous |
| [`relimp_network()`](https://pak.dynasite.org/psychnets/reference/relimp_network.md) | relative-importance network (LMG / Shapley) | continuous |
| [`ising_fit()`](https://pak.dynasite.org/psychnets/reference/ising_fit.md) | Ising model, L1-penalized | binary |
| [`ising_sampler()`](https://pak.dynasite.org/psychnets/reference/ising_sampler.md) | Ising model, unregularized | binary |
| [`mgm_fit()`](https://pak.dynasite.org/psychnets/reference/mgm_fit.md) | mixed graphical model | Gaussian + binary |
| [`psychnet()`](https://pak.dynasite.org/psychnets/reference/psychnet.md) | unified front door routing all of the above | — |

The framework verbs operate on any fitted network, and on event logs and
grouped data through
[`psychnet()`](https://pak.dynasite.org/psychnets/reference/psychnet.md):

| Verb | Purpose |
|----|----|
| [`net_centralities()`](https://pak.dynasite.org/psychnets/reference/net_centralities.md), [`net_bridge()`](https://pak.dynasite.org/psychnets/reference/net_bridge.md), [`net_edge_betweenness()`](https://pak.dynasite.org/psychnets/reference/net_edge_betweenness.md) | node, bridge, and edge centrality |
| [`net_clustering()`](https://pak.dynasite.org/psychnets/reference/net_clustering.md), [`net_smallworld()`](https://pak.dynasite.org/psychnets/reference/net_smallworld.md) | weighted clustering coefficients and the small-world index |
| [`net_predict()`](https://pak.dynasite.org/psychnets/reference/net_predict.md) | node predictability (variance explained; classification accuracy) |
| [`net_boot()`](https://pak.dynasite.org/psychnets/reference/net_boot.md), [`difference_test()`](https://pak.dynasite.org/psychnets/reference/difference_test.md) | bootstrapped accuracy and within-network difference tests |
| [`net_stability()`](https://pak.dynasite.org/psychnets/reference/net_stability.md), [`casedrop_reliability()`](https://pak.dynasite.org/psychnets/reference/casedrop_reliability.md), [`network_reliability()`](https://pak.dynasite.org/psychnets/reference/network_reliability.md) | case-dropping stability and split-half reliability |
| [`net_compare()`](https://pak.dynasite.org/psychnets/reference/net_compare.md) | the permutation Network Comparison Test |
| [`redundancy()`](https://pak.dynasite.org/psychnets/reference/redundancy.md), [`net_aggregate()`](https://pak.dynasite.org/psychnets/reference/net_aggregate.md) | redundant-node detection and community aggregation |

[`psychnet()`](https://pak.dynasite.org/psychnets/reference/psychnet.md)
additionally constructs networks directly from **event logs** — one row
per action — computing action frequencies and separating within- from
between-actor variation, and estimates a **separate network per group**
on request. Every fitted network inherits the `cograph` plotting
classes, and each result object carries a base-`graphics`
[`plot()`](https://rdrr.io/r/graphics/plot.default.html) method.

Node centrality plots directly from its result object:

``` r

plot(net_centralities(net))
```

![node centrality](reference/figures/README-centrality-1.png)

[`net_boot()`](https://pak.dynasite.org/psychnets/reference/net_boot.md)
resamples the data, re-estimates, and returns bootstrapped edge-weight
confidence intervals; edges whose interval excludes zero are drawn in
the accent colour:

``` r

set.seed(1)
plot(net_boot(SRL_GPT, method = "glasso", n_boot = 500), type = "edges")
```

![bootstrapped edge weights](reference/figures/README-bootstrap-1.png)

## Self-certification

`psychnets` treats correctness as a measured quantity rather than as
agreement with a second implementation. For a Gaussian graphical model,
[`glasso_kkt()`](https://pak.dynasite.org/psychnets/reference/glasso_kkt.md)
returns the stationarity residual of the graphical-lasso objective;
because that objective is strictly convex its minimizer is unique, so a
near-zero residual certifies the global optimum with no reference solver
involved. The nodewise Ising and mixed graphical models are certified
analogously through the penalized-likelihood score residual. External
packages (`qgraph`, `IsingFit`, `mgm`, `bootnet`) serve as cross-checks
at independent-solver precision, never as the definition of correct.

## Validation

`psychnets` is checked against the established reference packages in two
ways, both reproducible from a fixed set of package versions.

### Estimator equivalence versus the reference packages

Each model is fitted by `psychnets` and by the package that implements
it on the same data; equivalence is understood as *the same model, each
solved to its own optimum* — the edge set is identical and the residual
difference is at solver precision, while the `psychnets` KKT residual
certifies its own optimality.

| `psychnets` estimator | Reference package | Data | Observed difference |
|----|----|----|----|
| [`ebic_glasso()`](https://pak.dynasite.org/psychnets/reference/ebic_glasso.md) | [`qgraph::EBICglasso`](https://rdrr.io/pkg/qgraph/man/EBICglasso.html) 1.9.8 | AR(1) chain, *n* = 1000, *p* = 8 | edge set identical; max \|Δ\| = 2.2e-06; KKT = 1.5e-10 |
| [`ising_fit()`](https://pak.dynasite.org/psychnets/reference/ising_fit.md) | [`IsingFit::IsingFit`](https://rdrr.io/pkg/IsingFit/man/isingfit.html) 0.4 | AR(1) latent, *n* = 2000, *p* = 6, binarized | edge set identical; max \|Δ\| = 4.6e-03; threshold *r* = 1.000 |
| [`mgm_fit()`](https://pak.dynasite.org/psychnets/reference/mgm_fit.md) | [`mgm::mgm`](https://rdrr.io/pkg/mgm/man/mgm.html) 1.2.15 | mixed Gaussian + binary, *n* = 3000, *p* = 4 | edge set identical; max \|Δ\| = 1.6e-03; KKT = 1.1e-09 |
| [`cor_auto()`](https://pak.dynasite.org/psychnets/reference/cor_auto.md) | [`psych::polychoric`](https://rdrr.io/pkg/psych/man/tetrachor.html) / [`qgraph::cor_auto`](https://rdrr.io/pkg/qgraph/man/cor_auto.html) | four-level Likert, *n* = 1000, *p* = 4 | max \|Δ\| = 2.0e-05 (psych), 1.7e-07 (qgraph) |
| `.pbivnorm()` (bivariate normal CDF) | [`mvtnorm::pmvnorm`](https://rdrr.io/pkg/mvtnorm/man/pmvnorm.html) 1.2 | five (*h*, *k*, *ρ*) points | max \|Δ\| = 5.6e-17 |

### Validation databases (published instruments)

On real questionnaire and ability data the `psychnets` EBIC graphical
lasso reproduces the `qgraph` edge set exactly — structure agreement
equals 1 on every instrument, with a largest edge-weight difference of
0.008 — and the Ising model matches `IsingFit` on binary batteries. The
instruments, all loaded from installed CRAN packages so the comparison
downloads nothing:

| Instrument | Source | Items | *n* | Reference | max \|Δ\| |
|----|----|----|----|----|----|
| [`qgraph::big5`](https://rdrr.io/pkg/qgraph/man/big5.html) | Dolan, Oort, Stoel & Wicherts (2009) | 240 | 500 | qgraph | 3.6e-05 |
| [`psych::bfi`](https://rdrr.io/pkg/psych/man/bfi.html) | Revelle, Wilt & Rosenthal (2010) | 25 | 2760 | qgraph | 3e-06 |
| [`psych::sat.act`](https://rdrr.io/pkg/psych/man/sat.act.html) | Revelle (psych) | 6 | 700 | qgraph | 2e-06 |
| `psychTools::msq` | Revelle & Anderson (1998) | 50 | 3876 | qgraph | 1.2e-05 |
| `psychTools::epi.bfi` | Eysenck & Eysenck (1964) | 13 | 231 | qgraph | 8.0e-03 |
| `psychTools::epi` | Eysenck Personality Inventory | 50 | 3476 | qgraph | 6e-06 |
| `psychTools::sai` | Spielberger State Anxiety | 22 | 5282 | qgraph | 3.5e-05 |
| `psychTools::tai` | Spielberger Trait Anxiety | 21 | 3016 | qgraph | 7e-06 |
| `psychTools::spi` | Condon (2018), SAPA Inventory | 50 | 4000 | qgraph | 1.6e-05 |
| `psychTools::blot` | Bond’s Logical Operations Test | 35 | 150 | qgraph | 0 |
| [`mgm::Fried2015`](https://rdrr.io/pkg/mgm/man/datasets.html) | Fried et al. (2015) | 11 | 515 | qgraph | 8e-06 |
| [`mgm::PTSD_data`](https://rdrr.io/pkg/mgm/man/datasets.html) | McNally et al. (2015) | 6 | 344 | qgraph | 1.9e-05 |
| [`mgm::B5MS`](https://rdrr.io/pkg/mgm/man/datasets.html) | Haslbeck & Waldorp (mgm) | 5 | 500 | qgraph | 0 |
| [`mgm::symptom_data`](https://rdrr.io/pkg/mgm/man/datasets.html) | Haslbeck & Waldorp (mgm) | 48 | 1476 | qgraph | 1.5e-05 |
| `NetworkToolbox::neoOpen` | Christensen, Cotter & Silvia (2019) | 48 | 802 | qgraph | 4e-06 |
| [`networktools::depression`](https://rdrr.io/pkg/networktools/man/depression.html) | Jones (networktools) | 9 | 1000 | qgraph | 6.5e-05 |
| [`networktools::social`](https://rdrr.io/pkg/networktools/man/social.html) | Jones (networktools) | 16 | 350 | qgraph | 7e-06 |
| `EGAnet::optimism` | Golino & Christensen (EGAnet) | 10 | 272 | qgraph | 6e-06 |
| `EGAnet::intelligenceBattery` | Golino & Christensen (EGAnet) | 50 | 1152 | qgraph | 2.2e-05 |
| `psychTools::ability` | Revelle; ICAR ability items | 16 | 1248 | IsingFit | 5.9e-02 |
| `psychTools::iqitems` | Condon & Revelle; ICAR | 11 | 1523 | IsingFit | 1.2e-02 |

Where the truth is known — data drawn from a known sparse precision
matrix — the `psychnets` and `qgraph` estimators recover the generating
structure at the same F-measure. The full derivation, the worked
graphical-lasso case, and the complete tables are given in the package’s
technical report.

## Learn more

- **Articles** — worked tutorials for the Gaussian graphical,
  information-filtering, Ising, mixed, and relative-importance models,
  network reliability, and building networks from event data.
- **Website** — <https://pak.dynasite.org/psychnets>
- **Source** — <https://github.com/mohsaqr/psychnets>

# Package index

## All functions

- [`SRL_GPT`](https://pak.dynasite.org/psychnets/reference/SRL.md)
  [`SRL_Gemini`](https://pak.dynasite.org/psychnets/reference/SRL.md)
  [`SRL_Claude`](https://pak.dynasite.org/psychnets/reference/SRL.md)
  [`SRL_Mistral`](https://pak.dynasite.org/psychnets/reference/SRL.md)
  [`SRL_LLaMa`](https://pak.dynasite.org/psychnets/reference/SRL.md) :
  Self-regulated-learning (MSLQ) construct scores simulated by large
  language models
- [`as.data.frame(`*`<psychnet>`*`)`](https://pak.dynasite.org/psychnets/reference/as.data.frame.psychnet.md)
  : Tidy edge list for a psychnet network
- [`as.data.frame(`*`<psychnet_bootstrap>`*`)`](https://pak.dynasite.org/psychnets/reference/as.data.frame.psychnet_bootstrap.md)
  : Tidy a network bootstrap
- [`casedrop_reliability()`](https://pak.dynasite.org/psychnets/reference/casedrop_reliability.md)
  : Edge-weight stability coefficient (case-dropping subset bootstrap)
- [`` `$`( ``*`<psychnet>`*`)`](https://pak.dynasite.org/psychnets/reference/cash-.psychnet.md)
  : Back-compatible field access for a psychnet object
- [`certificate()`](https://pak.dynasite.org/psychnets/reference/certificate.md)
  : Correctness certificate of a fitted network
- [`condition()`](https://pak.dynasite.org/psychnets/reference/condition.md)
  : Condition a moderated network at a moderator value
- [`cor_auto()`](https://pak.dynasite.org/psychnets/reference/cor_auto.md)
  : Automatic correlation matrix (polychoric / polyserial / Pearson)
- [`cor_network()`](https://pak.dynasite.org/psychnets/reference/cor_network.md)
  : Correlation network
- [`dichotomize()`](https://pak.dynasite.org/psychnets/reference/dichotomize.md)
  : Dichotomize numeric columns to 0/1
- [`difference_test()`](https://pak.dynasite.org/psychnets/reference/difference_test.md)
  : Bootstrapped difference test for edges or centralities
- [`ebic_glasso()`](https://pak.dynasite.org/psychnets/reference/ebic_glasso.md)
  : EBIC-regularized Gaussian graphical model (graphical lasso)
- [`event_frequencies()`](https://pak.dynasite.org/psychnets/reference/event_frequencies.md)
  : Action frequencies from an event log
- [`ggm_modselect()`](https://pak.dynasite.org/psychnets/reference/ggm_modselect.md)
  : Stepwise Gaussian graphical model selection (ggmModSelect)
- [`ggm_support_kkt()`](https://pak.dynasite.org/psychnets/reference/ggm_support_kkt.md)
  : Constrained Gaussian-MRF (graph-restricted MLE) stationarity
  residual
- [`glasso_kkt()`](https://pak.dynasite.org/psychnets/reference/glasso_kkt.md)
  : Graphical-lasso stationarity (KKT) residual
- [`glm_lasso_kkt()`](https://pak.dynasite.org/psychnets/reference/glm_lasso_kkt.md)
  : Stationarity (KKT) residual of an L1-penalized GLM fit
- [`huge_network()`](https://pak.dynasite.org/psychnets/reference/huge_network.md)
  : Nonparanormal graphical model (huge)
- [`ising_fit()`](https://pak.dynasite.org/psychnets/reference/ising_fit.md)
  : Ising network for binary data
- [`ising_sampler()`](https://pak.dynasite.org/psychnets/reference/ising_sampler.md)
  : Unregularized Ising network for binary data
- [`lmg_certificate()`](https://pak.dynasite.org/psychnets/reference/lmg_certificate.md)
  : Relative-importance (LMG / Shapley) certificate
- [`logo_network()`](https://pak.dynasite.org/psychnets/reference/logo_network.md)
  : Local-Global sparse inverse covariance (LoGo)
- [`mgm_fit()`](https://pak.dynasite.org/psychnets/reference/mgm_fit.md)
  : Mixed graphical model
- [`net_aggregate()`](https://pak.dynasite.org/psychnets/reference/net_aggregate.md)
  : Aggregate a network's communities into super-nodes
- [`net_boot()`](https://pak.dynasite.org/psychnets/reference/net_boot.md)
  : Bootstrap a psychometric network
- [`net_bridge()`](https://pak.dynasite.org/psychnets/reference/net_bridge.md)
  : Bridge centrality
- [`net_centralities()`](https://pak.dynasite.org/psychnets/reference/net_centralities.md)
  : Node centrality
- [`net_clustering()`](https://pak.dynasite.org/psychnets/reference/net_clustering.md)
  : Weighted clustering coefficients
- [`net_compare()`](https://pak.dynasite.org/psychnets/reference/net_compare.md)
  : Network Comparison Test
- [`net_crosswalk()`](https://pak.dynasite.org/psychnets/reference/net_crosswalk.md)
  : Argument crosswalk: psychnet as a substitute for qgraph / IsingFit /
  mgm
- [`net_edge_betweenness()`](https://pak.dynasite.org/psychnets/reference/net_edge_betweenness.md)
  : Edge betweenness centrality
- [`net_predict()`](https://pak.dynasite.org/psychnets/reference/net_predict.md)
  : Node predictability
- [`net_smallworld()`](https://pak.dynasite.org/psychnets/reference/net_smallworld.md)
  : Small-world index
- [`net_stability()`](https://pak.dynasite.org/psychnets/reference/net_stability.md)
  : Centrality-stability coefficient (case-dropping subset bootstrap)
- [`network_reliability()`](https://pak.dynasite.org/psychnets/reference/network_reliability.md)
  : Split-half reliability of the network edge structure
- [`node_predictability()`](https://pak.dynasite.org/psychnets/reference/node_predictability.md)
  : Node predictability as a plotting vector
- [`pcor_network()`](https://pak.dynasite.org/psychnets/reference/pcor_network.md)
  : Partial correlation network
- [`plot(`*`<psychnet>`*`)`](https://pak.dynasite.org/psychnets/reference/plot.psychnet.md)
  : Plot a psychnet network
- [`plot(`*`<psychnet_bootstrap>`*`)`](https://pak.dynasite.org/psychnets/reference/plot.psychnet_bootstrap.md)
  : Plot a network bootstrap
- [`plot(`*`<psychnet_bridge>`*`)`](https://pak.dynasite.org/psychnets/reference/plot.psychnet_bridge.md)
  : Plot bridge centrality
- [`plot(`*`<psychnet_casedrop>`*`)`](https://pak.dynasite.org/psychnets/reference/plot.psychnet_casedrop.md)
  : Plot edge-weight case-dropping stability
- [`plot(`*`<psychnet_centrality>`*`)`](https://pak.dynasite.org/psychnets/reference/plot.psychnet_centrality.md)
  : Plot node centralities
- [`plot(`*`<psychnet_difference>`*`)`](https://pak.dynasite.org/psychnets/reference/plot.psychnet_difference.md)
  : Plot a bootstrapped difference test
- [`plot(`*`<psychnet_nct>`*`)`](https://pak.dynasite.org/psychnets/reference/plot.psychnet_nct.md)
  : Plot a Network Comparison Test
- [`plot(`*`<psychnet_reliability>`*`)`](https://pak.dynasite.org/psychnets/reference/plot.psychnet_reliability.md)
  : Plot split-half reliability
- [`plot(`*`<psychnet_stability>`*`)`](https://pak.dynasite.org/psychnets/reference/plot.psychnet_stability.md)
  : Plot centrality stability (case-dropping)
- [`print(`*`<psychnet>`*`)`](https://pak.dynasite.org/psychnets/reference/print.psychnet.md)
  : Print a psychnet network
- [`print(`*`<psychnet_bootstrap>`*`)`](https://pak.dynasite.org/psychnets/reference/print.psychnet_bootstrap.md)
  : Print a network bootstrap
- [`print(`*`<psychnet_casedrop>`*`)`](https://pak.dynasite.org/psychnets/reference/print.psychnet_casedrop.md)
  : Print an edge-weight stability result
- [`print(`*`<psychnet_group>`*`)`](https://pak.dynasite.org/psychnets/reference/print.psychnet_group.md)
  [`as.data.frame(`*`<psychnet_group>`*`)`](https://pak.dynasite.org/psychnets/reference/print.psychnet_group.md)
  [`summary(`*`<psychnet_group>`*`)`](https://pak.dynasite.org/psychnets/reference/print.psychnet_group.md)
  : Per-group psychometric networks
- [`print(`*`<psychnet_moderated>`*`)`](https://pak.dynasite.org/psychnets/reference/print.psychnet_moderated.md)
  : Print a moderated MGM fit
- [`print(`*`<psychnet_multilevel>`*`)`](https://pak.dynasite.org/psychnets/reference/print.psychnet_multilevel.md)
  [`as.data.frame(`*`<psychnet_multilevel>`*`)`](https://pak.dynasite.org/psychnets/reference/print.psychnet_multilevel.md)
  : Within / between event-data networks
- [`print(`*`<psychnet_nct>`*`)`](https://pak.dynasite.org/psychnets/reference/print.psychnet_nct.md)
  : Print a Network Comparison Test
- [`print(`*`<psychnet_redundancy>`*`)`](https://pak.dynasite.org/psychnets/reference/print.psychnet_redundancy.md)
  : Print a redundancy result
- [`print(`*`<psychnet_reliability>`*`)`](https://pak.dynasite.org/psychnets/reference/print.psychnet_reliability.md)
  : Print a split-half reliability result
- [`print(`*`<psychnet_result_group>`*`)`](https://pak.dynasite.org/psychnets/reference/print.psychnet_result_group.md)
  [`as.data.frame(`*`<psychnet_result_group>`*`)`](https://pak.dynasite.org/psychnets/reference/print.psychnet_result_group.md)
  : Per-group framework results
- [`print(`*`<psychnet_stability>`*`)`](https://pak.dynasite.org/psychnets/reference/print.psychnet_stability.md)
  : Print a centrality-stability result
- [`psychnet()`](https://pak.dynasite.org/psychnets/reference/psychnet.md)
  : Estimate a psychometric network
- [`redundancy()`](https://pak.dynasite.org/psychnets/reference/redundancy.md)
  : Detect redundant node pairs ("goldbricker")
- [`relimp_network()`](https://pak.dynasite.org/psychnets/reference/relimp_network.md)
  : Relative-importance network (LMG / Shapley)
- [`summary(`*`<psychnet>`*`)`](https://pak.dynasite.org/psychnets/reference/summary.psychnet.md)
  : Summarize a psychnet network
- [`tmfg_certificate()`](https://pak.dynasite.org/psychnets/reference/tmfg_certificate.md)
  : Structural certificate for a TMFG network
- [`tmfg_network()`](https://pak.dynasite.org/psychnets/reference/tmfg_network.md)
  : Triangulated Maximally Filtered Graph (TMFG)

# Articles

### All vignettes

- [Gaussian graphical models: an extended
  tutorial](https://pak.dynasite.org/psychnets/articles/gaussian-graphical-models-v2.md):
- [Gaussian graphical
  models](https://pak.dynasite.org/psychnets/articles/gaussian-graphical-models.md):
- [Stepwise unregularized
  GGM](https://pak.dynasite.org/psychnets/articles/ggm-model-selection.md):
- [Regulation networks from event
  data](https://pak.dynasite.org/psychnets/articles/group-networks-from-event-data.md):
- [Information-filtering networks: TMFG and
  LoGo](https://pak.dynasite.org/psychnets/articles/information-filtering-networks.md):
- [Ising networks for binary
  data](https://pak.dynasite.org/psychnets/articles/ising-networks.md):
- [Mixed graphical
  models](https://pak.dynasite.org/psychnets/articles/mixed-graphical-models.md):
- [Edge-weight stability and split-half
  reliability](https://pak.dynasite.org/psychnets/articles/network-reliability.md):
- [Relative-importance
  networks](https://pak.dynasite.org/psychnets/articles/relative-importance-networks.md):
- [Visualizing bootstrap, centrality and difference results (base
  R)](https://pak.dynasite.org/psychnets/articles/visualizing-networks.md):
- [Visualizing networks with
  cograph](https://pak.dynasite.org/psychnets/articles/visualizing-with-cograph.md):
